summary: Guardian Page persistent mount feature VERIFIED. Chat view persists across navigation (Guardian → Dashboard → Guardian). Implementation uses lazy loading + display:none pattern in DashboardLayout to keep GuardianPage mounted after first visit.
passed_tests: Login with credentials info@carryon.us / Demo1234! - successful (admin user redirects to /admin)
Navigate to Guardian page (/guardian) - Estate Guardian component loads correctly
Guardian landing page shows: Hero title 'Estate Guardian AI (EGA)', Recent Conversations, Quick Actions (Analyze Vault, Generate Checklist, Readiness Score, Beneficiary Review), Landing input
Click 'Analyze Vault' quick action - switches to chat view with thinking indicator ('Reading your documents...')
Navigate to Dashboard while analysis in progress - page loads correctly without freeze
CRITICAL: Navigate back to Guardian - Chat view PERSISTED with chat area visible and back button visible
Back button in chat view - returns to landing page correctly
Chat messages persist (found 1 message after return)

persistent_mount_architecture.details: DashboardLayout lazy-imports GuardianPage and renders it outside <Outlet/>. guardianMounted state tracks first visit. Display:none/block controls visibility based on route. Component stays mounted after first visit.

quick_action_chat_persistence.details: Clicking Analyze Vault → navigating to Dashboard → returning to Guardian shows chat view with active analysis (thinking indicator visible), NOT the landing page

back_button_functionality.details: Back button correctly clears session state and returns to landing view

dashboard_navigation_no_freeze.details: Dashboard page loads correctly after navigating away from active Guardian analysis

DashboardLayout.js.lines_9-19: Lazy import of GuardianPage, isOnGuardian check via location.pathname, guardianMounted state with useEffect to track first visit
DashboardLayout.js.lines_92-99: Persistent Guardian render: conditionally renders when guardianMounted=true, uses display:none/block based on isOnGuardian
DashboardLayout.js.assessment: CORRECT implementation - component stays mounted via display:none pattern instead of unmounting

App.js_line_261.description: Guardian Route renders null (placeholder) since actual component is rendered persistently in DashboardLayout
App.js_line_261.assessment: CORRECT - Route acts as URL trigger while component lives elsewhere

GuardianPage.js.key_features: Manages landing vs chat view state internally, localStorage for session persistence, session ID management
GuardianPage.js.assessment: WORKING - component maintains state across hide/show via display:none

test_screenshots: .screenshots/02_guardian_landing.png - Guardian landing with Quick Actions and Recent Conversations
.screenshots/03_chat_analyze.png - Chat view with Analyze Vault in progress (thinking indicator)
.screenshots/05_guardian_return_CRITICAL.png - CRITICAL: Chat view persisted with 'Reading your documents...' thinking indicator visible

test_credentials.note: Admin user - redirects to /admin then can access /guardian

context_for_next_testing_agent: Guardian page persistent mount feature is working. Architecture: 1) Route renders null 2) DashboardLayout lazy imports and persistently renders GuardianPage 3) display:none/block controls visibility 4) guardianMounted state ensures component only loads after first /guardian visit. Rate limiting (429) observed during testing - may need to add delays between API calls in future tests.
environment_notes: Server has rate limiting that can cause 429 errors with rapid API calls. Allow 5-10 second delays between major navigations during testing.
